name: model_business_rules
description: Record and manage business rules for a project. Rules are classified as operational (process/behaviour rules), structural (constraints on data or relationships), or derivation (computed values or inferred facts). Supports add, update, list (with filters), free-text search, and deprecation. Use this when the user wants to capture, review, or maintain the rules that govern a business domain.

action.description: 'add' a new rule, 'update' an existing rule by rule_id, 'list' rules with optional filters, 'search' by free text, 'deprecate' sets the rule status to deprecated.

user_id.description: User ID from session context. Always required.

project.description: Project scope. Used as filter in list and search.

rule_id.description: UUID of the business rule. Required for update and deprecate.

rule_name.description: Short descriptive name for the rule. Required for add.

rule_type.description: Category of rule. Required for add.

trigger_condition.description: Event or condition that activates this rule (e.g. 'when order is placed').

condition_text.description: The IF clause — conditions that must be true for the rule to apply.

action_text.description: The THEN clause — what must happen when the condition is met.

exception_text.description: Known exceptions or edge cases where the rule does not apply.

source_requirement_id.description: ID of the source requirement this rule was derived from.

owner.description: Business owner or SME responsible for this rule.

status.description: Current status of the rule. Filter for list action.

query.description: Free text to search across rule_name, condition_text, and action_text. Required for search.

record.description: Full rule record after the operation (add/update).
